supplier_name: Health & Care Innovations Ltd / University of Exeter & Plymouth
one_line_value_proposition: NICE HTG764-recommended digital adaptation of the NIHR-funded REACH-HF programme — the only heart failure-specific CR platform with a multicentre RCT evidence base and built-in caregiver support.
why_it_matters_locally: Heart failure patients are significantly underserved by conventional cardiac rehabilitation. D REACH-HF provides a home-based, facilitated 12-week programme specifically designed for heart failure (HFrEF and HFpEF), with a unique Family and Friends Resource for caregivers. The paper-based REACH-HF programme has strong NIHR-funded RCT evidence showing superior quality of life outcomes.
target_problem_statement: Very low CR uptake in heart failure patients, limited HF-specific CR programmes, and lack of structured support for caregivers of heart failure patients.

nice_guidance_refs.note: Conditionally recommended — evidence for non-digital REACH-HF; digital version evidence generation underway

evidence_summary: Strong evidence for the paper-based REACH-HF programme: NIHR-funded multicentre RCT (HFrEF) showing clinically superior disease-specific HRQoL at 12 months vs usual care, cost-effective within NHS tariff. Pilot RCT (HFpEF) demonstrated feasibility with favourable caregiver impacts. SCOT:REACH-HF implementation study across six NHS Scotland Health Boards replicated RCT findings. Digital version (D REACH-HF) has a peer-reviewed development paper but no completed RCT yet — evidence generation during the 3-year NICE period will address this.

case_studies.title: SCOT:REACH-HF implementation study
case_studies.outcome: Substantive HRQoL and self-management improvements, replicating RCT findings in real-world implementation.
case_studies.caveat: Paper-based version, not the digital adaptation. Published in European Journal of Cardiovascular Nursing (2023).

live_sites: Paper-based REACH-HF deployed across six NHS Scotland Health Boards and four NHS CR centres. D REACH-HF (digital) specific deployment sites not publicly confirmed.
patients_covered_note: Paper-based version deployed across multiple NHS sites. Digital version deployment scale not publicly confirmed.
dtac_note: Not within central DTAC records. DTAC approval is a condition of the NICE EVA recommendation.
device_class: Digital platform supporting CR — assessed under NICE EVA
cyber_notes: Developed as part of NIHR-funded research programme with full ethical approvals and governance. Confirm current assurance status.
gdpr_note: Confirm data hosting and DPA terms with Health & Care Innovations Ltd.
target_patients: Adults with heart failure (HFrEF and HFpEF) suitable for home-based cardiac rehabilitation.
exclusions: Clinical assessment required. Not suitable for patients requiring supervised exercise or acute heart failure management.
onboarding_detail: 12-week facilitated programme. Three healthcare professionals per site trained to deliver. Typically 2-4 face-to-face sessions (home/clinic) plus 2-4 telephone consultations. Training covers intervention manual, facilitation skills, and digital platform use.
supplier_wrap: Healthcare professional dashboard with remote access to patient-entered data. Caregiver interface via Progress Tracker.
local_wraparound_detail: Trained facilitators (typically CR nurses or physiotherapists) required — three per site. Initial face-to-face clinical assessment needed. Implementation model follows Normalisation Process Theory.
training_note: Facilitator training covering intervention manual, facilitation skills, and digital platform use is mandatory.
monitoring_model: Healthcare professional dashboard monitors patient engagement, progress, and needs during the 12-week facilitation period. Not continuously monitored.
monitoring_note: Facilitated programme with structured check-ins. HCP dashboard shows patient-entered data. Not a continuously monitored service.
escalation_note: Facilitators manage clinical escalation within the CR service. Standard NHS escalation routes for deterioration.
operating_hours_caveat: Not a monitored emergency service. 12-week facilitated programme with structured contact points.
indicative_price_text: Information not available. Contact Health & Care Innovations Ltd for NHS pricing.
service_wrap_note: Facilitator training and intervention manual provided. Clinical delivery is commissioner/provider responsibility.
contract_note: Information not available. NICE advises considering the 3-year evidence generation period.
expected_benefit_note: Original REACH-HF RCT showed clinically superior disease-specific quality of life at 12 months vs usual care. Cost-effective within NHS tariff. NICE EAG model found cost-saving vs conventional rehabilitation.
tariff_considerations: Original REACH-HF programme cost-effective within NHS CR tariff. Model using HF-specific CR service costs.
roi_note: NICE EAG model found cost-saving vs conventional rehabilitation. NIHR-funded economic modelling supports cost-effectiveness.
minimum_conditions_for_success: Three trained facilitators per site, established HF CR referral pathway, capacity for face-to-face assessment and telephone consultations.
procurement_notes: Contact Health & Care Innovations Ltd. Confirm procurement route availability.

technical_integrations.device_integration: No specific device integration described. Web-based platform.
technical_integrations.data_hosting: Confirm with Health & Care Innovations Ltd

context_of_use.population: Adults with heart failure (HFrEF and HFpEF)
context_of_use.therapeutic_purpose: Structured 12-week facilitated programme: exercise, education, progress tracking, caregiver support via Family and Friends Resource
context_of_use.hcp_involvement: Active — trained facilitators deliver the programme with face-to-face and telephone contact
context_of_use.nice_scope: NICE HTG764 — conditionally recommended during 3-year evidence generation period

clinical_evidence_detailed.journal: NIHR Programme Grants for Applied Research
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Clinically superior disease-specific health-related quality of life (HRQoL) at 12 months vs usual care. Cost-effective within NHS tariff for cardiac rehabilitation.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Paper-based REACH-HF programme — not the digital adaptation (D REACH-HF). Results may not transfer directly to the digital version.
clinical_evidence_detailed.data_quality_note: NIHR-funded multicentre RCT. High-quality evidence but for the paper-based version.

clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Demonstrated feasibility and acceptability of REACH-HF for HFpEF. Favourable caregiver mental health impacts reported.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Pilot study — single centre, small sample. Paper-based version, not D REACH-HF. Powered for feasibility not efficacy.

clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Substantive HRQoL and self-management improvements replicated the original RCT findings in real-world NHS implementation across six Scottish Health Boards.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Paper-based REACH-HF programme — not the digital adaptation. Implementation study, not an RCT.

clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: Four UK NHS cardiac rehabilitation centres
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Published protocol for evaluating the implementation of REACH-HF across four NHS CR centres. Establishes framework for assessing real-world adoption.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Protocol paper — reports methodology, not clinical outcomes.

clinical_evidence_detailed.setting: Health & Care Innovations Ltd / University of Exeter & Plymouth
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Peer-reviewed description of iterative user-centred design of D REACH-HF using intervention mapping methodology. Documents the systematic translation from paper-based to digital format.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Development/design paper — describes the digital adaptation process, not clinical outcomes.

clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Paper and digital versions of REACH-HF adapted for the Danish healthcare context. Demonstrates international applicability of the REACH-HF model.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Adaptation study — reports on translation and cultural adaptation rather than new clinical outcomes.

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: NICE EVA HTG764 — Digital platforms to support cardiac rehabilitation
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: Conditionally recommended. NICE noted evidence of clinical benefit for the non-digital REACH-HF but no direct evidence for the digital version. EAG model found cost-saving vs conventional rehabilitation. Uncertainty to be addressed through evidence generation.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Conditional recommendation — 3-year evidence generation period required. DTAC approval must be obtained. No completed RCT for the digital version specifically.

clinical_evidence_detailed.ref: D REACH-HF digital version — evidence gap
clinical_evidence_detailed.key_results: No completed RCT evidence for the digital version (D REACH-HF) specifically. All clinical outcome evidence relates to the paper-based REACH-HF programme. Evidence generation during the 3-year NICE period will address this gap. Full multicentre RCT (REACH-HFpEF) underway for HFpEF population.
clinical_evidence_detailed.study_limitation: Critical gap — commissioners should note that all clinical outcome evidence relates to the paper-based predecessor, not the digital product.
clinical_evidence_detailed.data_quality_note: No direct clinical outcome evidence for the digital version. Evidence generation is a condition of the NICE EVA recommendation.

demo_notes: Contact Health & Care Innovations Ltd for further information.
source_summary: NICE HTG764, NIHR REACH-HF programme, Dalal et al. 2021, van Beurden et al. 2025, SCOT:REACH-HF (EJCN 2023).
confidence_note: Strong evidence exists for the paper-based REACH-HF programme. The digital adaptation (D REACH-HF) does not yet have completed RCT evidence. DTAC approval must be obtained. Verify all details before procurement.
